name: DateField
description: Allow users to select a specific date with a date picker.

properties.name: allow
properties.description:
Dates that can be selected.

A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` allows all dates.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format allow a single date.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM` format allow a whole month.

* Dates in `YYYY` format allow a whole year.

* Ranges are expressed as `start--end`. - Ranges are inclusive.

* If either `start` or `end` is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `start`, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So `2024--` is equivalent to `2024-01-01--`.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `end`, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So `--2024` is equivalent to `--2024-12-31`.
* Whitespace is allowed either side of `--`.

properties.name: allowDays
properties.description:
Days of the week that can be selected. These intersect with the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

Days are `sunday`, `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: defaultView
properties.description:
Default month to display in `YYYY-MM` format.

This value is used until `view` is set, either directly or as a result of user interaction.

Defaults to the current month in the user's locale.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: disallow
properties.description:
Dates that cannot be selected. These subtract from `allow`.

A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on `allow`.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format disallow a single date.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM` format disallow a whole month.

* Dates in `YYYY` format disallow a whole year.

* Ranges are expressed as `start--end`. - Ranges are inclusive.

* If either `start` or `end` is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `start`, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So `2024--` is equivalent to `2024-01-01--`.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `end`, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So `--2024` is equivalent to `--2024-12-31`.
* Whitespace is allowed either side of `--`.

properties.name: disallowDays
properties.description:
Days of the week that cannot be selected. This subtracts from `allowDays`, and intersects with the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on `allowDays`.

Days are `sunday`, `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

properties.name: view
properties.description:
Displayed month in `YYYY-MM` format.

`onViewChange` is called when this value changes.

Defaults to `defaultView`.

bestPractices: Use smart defaults and highlight common selections
Use `allow` and `disallow` properties to restrict selectable dates appropriately
Provide clear labels and use details text to explain date restrictions
Don't use for dates that are many years in the future or the past

name: DatePicker
description: Allow users to select a specific date or date range.

properties.name: allow
properties.description:
Dates that can be selected.

A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` allows all dates.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format allow a single date.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM` format allow a whole month.

* Dates in `YYYY` format allow a whole year.

* Ranges are expressed as `start--end`. - Ranges are inclusive.

* If either `start` or `end` is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `start`, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So `2024--` is equivalent to `2024-01-01--`.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `end`, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So `--2024` is equivalent to `--2024-12-31`.
* Whitespace is allowed either side of `--`.

properties.name: allowDays
properties.description:
Days of the week that can be selected. These intersect with the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

Days are `sunday`, `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description:
Default selected value.

The default means no date is selected.

If the provided value is invalid, no date is selected.

* If `type="single"`, this is a date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
* If `type="multiple"`, this is a comma-separated list of dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
* If `type="range"`, this is a range in `YYYY-MM-DD--YYYY-MM-DD` format. The range is inclusive.

properties.name: defaultView
properties.description:
Default month to display in `YYYY-MM` format.

This value is used until `view` is set, either directly or as a result of user interaction.

Defaults to the current month in the user's locale.

properties.name: disallow
properties.description:
Dates that cannot be selected. These subtract from `allow`.

A comma-separated list of dates, date ranges. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on `allow`.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format disallow a single date.

* Dates in `YYYY-MM` format disallow a whole month.

* Dates in `YYYY` format disallow a whole year.

* Ranges are expressed as `start--end`. - Ranges are inclusive.

* If either `start` or `end` is omitted, the range is unbounded in that direction.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `start`, they are assumed to be the minimum possible value. So `2024--` is equivalent to `2024-01-01--`.
* If parts of the date are omitted for `end`, they are assumed to be the maximum possible value. So `--2024` is equivalent to `--2024-12-31`.
* Whitespace is allowed either side of `--`.

properties.name: disallowDays
properties.description:
Days of the week that cannot be selected. This subtracts from `allowDays`, and intersects with the result of `allow` and `disallow`.

A comma-separated list of days. Whitespace is allowed after commas.

The default `''` has no effect on `allowDays`.

Days are `sunday`, `monday`, `tuesday`, `wednesday`, `thursday`, `friday`, `saturday`.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: value
properties.description:
Current selected value.

The default means no date is selected.

If the provided value is invalid, no date is selected.

Otherwise:

* If `type="single"`, this is a date in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
* If `type="multiple"`, this is a comma-separated list of dates in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
* If `type="range"`, this is a range in `YYYY-MM-DD--YYYY-MM-DD` format. The range is inclusive.

properties.name: view
properties.description:
Displayed month in `YYYY-MM` format.

`onViewChange` is called when this value changes.

Defaults to `defaultView`.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-date-picker
    view="2025-05"
    type="range"
    value="2025-05-28--2025-05-31"
   />
examples.html:
<s-date-picker
    view="2025-05"
    type="range"
    value="2025-05-28--2025-05-31"
  ></s-date-picker>

examples.title: Single date selection
examples.description: Demonstrates a date picker configured for selecting a single date with a default value and specific month view.
examples.jsx:
<s-date-picker
    type="single"
    name="delivery-date"
    value="2024-01-15"
    view="2024-01"
   />
examples.html:
<s-date-picker
    type="single"
    name="delivery-date"
    value="2024-01-15"
    view="2024-01"
  ></s-date-picker>

examples.title: With date restrictions
examples.description: Illustrates how to restrict date selection to a specific date range, preventing selection of past or future dates outside the allowed period.
examples.jsx:
<s-date-picker
    type="single"
    name="appointment-date"
    disallow="past"
    allow="2024-06-01--2024-06-31"
    view="2024-06"
   />
examples.html:
<!-- Disable past dates and far future dates -->
  <s-date-picker
    type="single"
    name="appointment-date"
    disallow="past"
    allow="2024-06-01--2024-06-31"
    view="2024-06"
  ></s-date-picker>

examples.title: Handling onChange callbacks
examples.description: Demonstrates how to handle onChange callbacks for both single and range date pickers, showing how to extract and process the selected values.
examples.html:
<form>
    <s-text-field
      label="Order number"
      placeholder="Search orders..."
    ></s-text-field>
    <s-date-picker
      type="range"
      name="order-date-range"
      value="2024-01-01--2024-01-31"
      view="2024-01"
    ></s-date-picker>
    <s-button type="submit">Apply filters</s-button>
  </form>

examples.title: With quick date selection
examples.description: Illustrates a date picker with quick preset buttons and onChange callback to capture user selections and update the displayed value.
examples.html:
<!-- Quick date selection with onChange callback -->
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <s-button-group>
      <s-button slot="secondary-actions" id="last-7-days">Last 7 days</s-button>
      <s-button slot="secondary-actions" id="last-30-days">Last 30 days</s-button>
      <s-button slot="secondary-actions" id="this-month">This month</s-button>
    </s-button-group>
    <s-date-picker
      type="range"
      name="analytics-period"
      id="analytics-picker"
      value="2025-01-01--2025-01-31"
      view="2025-01"
      onchange="console.log('Date range changed:', event.currentTarget.value)"
    ></s-date-picker>
    <s-text id="selected-range">
      Selected range: 2025-01-01--2025-01-31
    </s-text>
  </s-stack>

  <script>
    const picker = document.getElementById('analytics-picker');
    const display = document.getElementById('selected-range');

    // Handle picker changes
    picker.addEventListener('change', (event) => {
      display.textContent = `Selected range: ${event.currentTarget.value}`;
    });

    // Quick selection buttons
    document.getElementById('last-7-days').addEventListener('click', () => {
      picker.value = '2025-01-07--2025-01-13';
      display.textContent = 'Selected range: 2025-01-07--2025-01-13';
    });

    document.getElementById('last-30-days').addEventListener('click', () => {
      picker.value = '2024-12-14--2025-01-13';
      display.textContent = 'Selected range: 2024-12-14--2025-01-13';
    });

    document.getElementById('this-month').addEventListener('click', () => {
      picker.value = '2025-01-01--2025-01-31';
      display.textContent = 'Selected range: 2025-01-01--2025-01-31';
    });
  </script>

bestPractices: Use smart defaults and highlight common selections
Don't use to enter a date that is many years in the future or the past

name: DropZone
description: Lets users upload files through drag-and-drop functionality into a designated area on a page, or by activating a button.

properties.name: accept
properties.description:
A string representing the types of files that are accepted by the drop zone. This string is a comma-separated list of unique file type specifiers which can be one of the following:

* A file extension starting with a period (".") character (e.g. .jpg, .pdf, .doc)
* A valid MIME type string with no extensions

If omitted, all file types are accepted.

properties.name: accessibilityLabel
properties.description: A label that describes the purpose or contents of the item. When set, it will be announced to buyers using assistive technologies and will provide them with more context.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: files
properties.description:
An array of File objects representing the files currently selected by the user.

This property is read-only and cannot be directly modified. To clear the selected files, set the `value` prop to an empty string or null.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: multiple
properties.description: Whether multiple files can be selected or dropped at once.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: This sets the input value for a file type, which cannot be set programatically, so it can only be reset.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-drop-zone
    label="Upload"
    accessibilityLabel="Upload image of type jpg, png, or gif"
    accept=".jpg,.png,.gif"
    multiple
    onInput={(event) => console.log('onInput', event.currentTarget?.value)}
    onChange={(event) => console.log('onChange', event.currentTarget?.value)}
    onDropRejected={(event) => console.log('onDropRejected', event.currentTarget?.value)}
   />
examples.html:
<s-drop-zone
    label="Upload"
    accessibilityLabel="Upload image of type jpg, png, or gif"
    accept=".jpg,.png,.gif"
    multiple
    onInput="console.log('onInput', event.currentTarget?.value)"
    onChange="console.log('onChange', event.currentTarget?.value)"
    onDropRejected="console.log('onDropRejected', event.currentTarget?.value)"
  ></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: Basic usage

examples.html: <s-drop-zone label="Drop files to upload" multiple></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: Image upload
examples.description: Shows a drop zone configured specifically for uploading multiple image files.
examples.jsx: <s-drop-zone accept="image/*" label="Upload images" multiple />
examples.html: <s-drop-zone accept="image/*" label="Upload images" multiple></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: With required field
examples.description: Illustrates a drop zone when the file upload is required.
examples.jsx: <s-drop-zone name="file" required label="Upload file" />
examples.html: <s-drop-zone name="file" required label="Upload file"></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: Disabled state

examples.html: <s-drop-zone label="Upload not available" disabled></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: File type restrictions
examples.description: Demonstrates restricting file uploads to specific document types like PDF and DOC.

    accept=".pdf,.doc,.docx"
    label="Upload documents"
    multiple
   />

examples.title: With error state

    label="Upload file"
    error="File size must be less than 5mb"
   />
examples.html:

    label="Upload file"
    error="File size must be less than 5mb"
  ></s-drop-zone>

examples.title: With accessibility options
examples.description: Illustrates advanced accessibility configuration for the drop zone, including custom accessibility labels.
examples.jsx:
<s-drop-zone
    label="Upload files"
    accessibilityLabel="Upload files using drag and drop or file selector"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    multiple
   />
examples.html:
<s-drop-zone
    label="Upload files"
    accessibilityLabel="Upload files using drag and drop or file selector"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    multiple
  ></s-drop-zone>

name: EmailField
description: Let users enter email addresses with optimized keyboard settings.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.type: "on" | "off" | EmailAutocompleteField | \`section-${string} email\` | \`section-${string} home email\` | \`section-${string} mobile email\` | \`section-${string} fax email\` | \`section-${string} pager email\` | "shipping email" | "shipping home email" | "shipping mobile email" | "shipping fax email" | "shipping pager email" | "billing email" | "billing home email" | "billing mobile email" | "billing fax email" | "billing pager email" | \`section-${string} shipping email\` | \`section-${string} shipping home email\` | \`section-${string} shipping mobile email\` | \`section-${string} shipping fax email\` | \`section-${string} shipping pager email\` | \`section-${string} billing email\` | \`section-${string} billing home email\` | \`section-${string} billing mobile email\` | \`section-${string} billing fax email\` | \`section-${string} billing pager email\`
properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: maxLength
properties.description: Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.

properties.name: minLength
properties.description: Specifies the min number of characters allowed.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-email-field
    label="Email"
    placeholder="bernadette.lapresse@jadedpixel.com"
    details="Used for sending notifications"
   />
examples.html:
<s-email-field
    label="Email"
    placeholder="bernadette.lapresse@jadedpixel.com"
    details="Used for sending notifications"
  ></s-email-field>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple email field with a label and required attribute, showing the most fundamental way to use the EmailField component.
examples.jsx:

    <s-email-field label="Email address" required />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field label="Email address" required></s-email-field>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: With error and help text
examples.description: Showcases an email field with additional details and an error message, illustrating how to provide contextual information and validation feedback.
examples.jsx:

    <s-email-field
      label="Contact email"
      details="We'll send your order confirmation here"
      error="Please enter a valid email address"
      required
    />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Contact email"
      details="We'll send your order confirmation here"

      required
    ></s-email-field>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: Optional field with placeholder
examples.description: Illustrates an optional email field with a placeholder text and help text, demonstrating a common pattern for collecting alternative contact information.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Alternate email"
      placeholder="secondary@example.com"
      details="Additional email for notifications"
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Alternate email"
      placeholder="secondary@example.com"
      details="Additional email for notifications"
    ></s-email-field>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: Read-only display
examples.description: Shows how to render an email field in a read-only state, useful for displaying existing email addresses that cannot be modified.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Account email"
      value="user@example.com"
      readOnly
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Account email"
      value="user@example.com"
      readOnly
    ></s-email-field>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: With length constraints
examples.description: Demonstrates setting minimum and maximum length constraints for the email input, providing additional validation beyond the standard email format check.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Business email"
      minLength={5}
      maxLength={100}
      required
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Business email"
      minLength="5"
      maxLength="100"
      required
    ></s-email-field>
  </s-stack>

name: MoneyField
description: Collect monetary values from users with built-in currency formatting and validation.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: max
properties.description:
The highest decimal or integer to be accepted for the field. When used with `step` the value will round down to the max number.

Note: a user will still be able to use the keyboard to input a number higher than the max. It is up to the developer to add appropriate validation.

properties.name: min
properties.description:
The lowest decimal or integer to be accepted for the field. When used with `step` the value will round up to the min number.

Note: a user will still be able to use the keyboard to input a number lower than the min. It is up to the developer to add appropriate validation.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

name: NumberField
description: Collect numerical values from users with optimized keyboard settings and built-in validation.

properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: inputMode
properties.description: Sets the virtual keyboard.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: max
properties.description:
The highest decimal or integer to be accepted for the field. When used with `step` the value will round down to the max number.

Note: a user will still be able to use the keyboard to input a number higher than the max. It is up to the developer to add appropriate validation.

properties.name: min
properties.description:
The lowest decimal or integer to be accepted for the field. When used with `step` the value will round up to the min number.

Note: a user will still be able to use the keyboard to input a number lower than the min. It is up to the developer to add appropriate validation.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: prefix
properties.description:
A value to be displayed immediately before the editable portion of the field.

This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "https://" or "+353".

This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field.

It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the prefix until the user focuses the input.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: step
properties.description: The amount the value can increase or decrease by. This can be an integer or decimal. If a `max` or `min` is specified with `step` when increasing/decreasing the value via the buttons, the final value will always round to the `max` or `min` rather than the closest valid amount.

properties.name: suffix
properties.description:
A value to be displayed immediately after the editable portion of the field.

This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "@shopify.com", or "%".

This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field.

It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the suffix until the user focuses the input.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-number-field
    label="Quantity"
    details="Number of items in stock"
    placeholder="0"
    step={5}
    min={0}
    max={100}
  />
examples.html:
<s-number-field
    label="Quantity"
    details="Number of items in stock"
    placeholder="0"
    step="5"
    min="0"
    max="100"
  ></s-number-field>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple number field for entering order quantity with a predefined range and step value.
examples.jsx:
<s-number-field
    label="Order quantity"
    value="5"
    min={1}
    max={999}
    step={1}
   />
examples.html:
<s-number-field
    label="Order quantity"
    value="5"
    min="1"
    max="999"
    step="1"
  ></s-number-field>

examples.title: With prefix and suffix
examples.description: Illustrates a number field for entering product prices with currency prefix and suffix, using decimal input mode.
examples.jsx:
<s-number-field
    label="Product price"
    value="29.99"
    prefix="$"
    suffix="CAD"
    inputMode="decimal"
    step={0.01}
    min={0}
   />
examples.html:
<s-number-field
    label="Product price"
    value="29.99"
    prefix="$"
    suffix="CAD"
    inputMode="decimal"
    step="0.01"
    min="0"
  ></s-number-field>

examples.title: Multiple examples
examples.description: Showcases multiple number fields for different use cases: inventory tracking, percentage discount, and shipping weight, demonstrating various input modes and configurations.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-number-field
      label="Inventory count"
      value="150"
      min={0}
      step={1}
      inputMode="numeric"
      details="Current stock available for sale"
     />

    <s-number-field
      label="Discount percentage"
      value="15"
      suffix="%"
      min={0}
      max={100}
      step={0.1}
      inputMode="decimal"
     />

    <s-number-field
      label="Shipping weight"
      value="2.5"
      suffix="kg"
      min={0.1}
      step={0.1}
      inputMode="decimal"
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-number-field
      label="Inventory count"
      value="150"
      min="0"
      step="1"
      inputMode="numeric"
      details="Current stock available for sale"
    ></s-number-field>

    <s-number-field
      label="Discount percentage"
      value="15"
      suffix="%"
      min="0"
      max="100"
      step="0.1"
      inputMode="decimal"
    ></s-number-field>

    <s-number-field
      label="Shipping weight"
      value="2.5"
      suffix="kg"
      min="0.1"
      step="0.1"
      inputMode="decimal"
    ></s-number-field>
  </s-stack>

name: PasswordField
description: Securely collect sensitive information from users.

properties.name: autocomplete
properties.type: "on" | "off" | PasswordAutocompleteField | \`section-${string} current-password\` | \`section-${string} new-password\` | "shipping current-password" | "shipping new-password" | "billing current-password" | "billing new-password" | \`section-${string} shipping current-password\` | \`section-${string} shipping new-password\` | \`section-${string} billing current-password\` | \`section-${string} billing new-password\`
properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: maxLength
properties.description: Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.

properties.name: minLength
properties.description: Specifies the min number of characters allowed.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    placeholder="Enter your password"
    details="Must be at least 8 characters long"
    minLength={8}
   />
examples.html:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    placeholder="Enter your password"
    details="Must be at least 8 characters long"
    minLength="8"
  ></s-password-field>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic password field with a label, name, and required validation. Sets a minimum length of 8 characters and configures autocomplete for a new password.
examples.jsx:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    name="password"
    required
    minLength={8}
    autocomplete="new-password"
  />
examples.html:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    name="password"
    required
    minLength="8"
    autocomplete="new-password"
  ></s-password-field>

examples.title: With error state
examples.description: Shows a password field in an error state, displaying a custom error message when the password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
examples.jsx:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    name="password"
    error="Password must be at least 8 characters"
    minLength={8}
    autocomplete="new-password"
   />
examples.html:
<s-password-field
    label="Password"
    name="password"
    error="Password must be at least 8 characters"
    minLength="8"
    autocomplete="new-password"
  ></s-password-field>

examples.title: With helper text
examples.description: Illustrates a password field with additional details providing guidance about password creation requirements.
examples.jsx:
<s-password-field
    label="Create password"
    name="new-password"
    details="Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, and numbers"
    minLength={8}
    autocomplete="new-password"
   />
examples.html:
<s-password-field
    label="Create password"
    name="new-password"
    details="Password must be at least 8 characters and include uppercase, lowercase, and numbers"
    minLength="8"
    autocomplete="new-password"
  ></s-password-field>

examples.title: In form layout
examples.description: Shows how the password field can be integrated into a form alongside other input fields, such as an email field, to create a complete login or registration form.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Email"
      name="email"
      autocomplete="email"
      required
     />
    <s-password-field
      label="Password"
      name="password"
      autocomplete="current-password"
      required
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-email-field
      label="Email"
      name="email"
      autocomplete="username"
      required
    ></s-email-field>
    <s-password-field
      label="Password"
      name="password"
      autocomplete="current-password"
      required
    ></s-password-field>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: With password strength requirements
examples.description: Demonstrates a password field with dynamic password strength validation, showing real-time feedback on password complexity requirements.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="large-100">
    <s-password-field
      label="Create password"
      name="password"
      value="example-password"
      autocomplete="new-password"
      required
     />
    <s-stack gap="small-200">
      <s-text tone="success">✓ At least 8 characters</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains uppercase letter</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains lowercase letter</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains number</s-text>
    </s-stack>
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="large-100">
    <s-password-field
      label="Create password"
      name="password"
      value="example-password"
      autocomplete="new-password"
      required
    ></s-password-field>
    <s-stack gap="small-200">
      <s-text tone="success">✓ At least 8 characters</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains uppercase letter</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains lowercase letter</s-text>
      <s-text color="subdued">○ Contains number</s-text>
    </s-stack>
  </s-stack>

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-search-field
    label="Search"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    placeholder="Search items"
   />
examples.html:
<s-search-field
    label="Search"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    placeholder="Search items"
  ></s-search-field>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a standard search input field for product discovery, with a clear label, name, and placeholder text to guide user interaction.
examples.jsx:
<s-search-field
    label="Search products"
    name="search"
    placeholder="Search products..."
   />
examples.html:
<s-search-field
    label="Search products"
    name="search"
    placeholder="Search products..."
  ></s-search-field>

    label="Search orders"
    name="orderSearch"
    error="No results found"
    value="xyz123"
   />
examples.html:
<s-search-field
    label="Search orders"
    name="orderSearch"
    error="No results found"
    value="xyz123"
  ></s-search-field>

examples.title: Disabled state

    name="customerSearch"
    disabled
    placeholder="Search is currently unavailable"

examples.html:

<s-search-field
    label="Search collections"
    name="collectionSearch"
    minLength={3}
    maxLength={50}
    placeholder="Enter at least 3 characters"
   />

<s-search-field
    label="Search collections"
    name="collectionSearch"
    minLength="3"
    maxLength="50"
    placeholder="Enter at least 3 characters"
  ></s-search-field>

bestPractices: The SearchField automatically includes a clear button when text is entered, so you should not create your own clear button

name: Select
description: Allow users to pick one option from a menu. Ideal when presenting four or more choices to keep interfaces uncluttered.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The type of icon to be displayed in the field.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

slots.name: children
slots.description:
The options a user can select from.

Accepts `Option` components.

Examples

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-select label="Date range">
    <s-option value="1">Today</s-option>
    <s-option value="2">Yesterday</s-option>
    <s-option value="3">Last 7 days</s-option>
    <s-option-group label="Custom ranges">
      <s-option value="4">Last 30 days</s-option>
      <s-option value="5">Last 90 days</s-option>
    </s-option-group>
  </s-select>
examples.html:
<s-select label="Date range">
    <s-option value="1">Today</s-option>
    <s-option value="2">Yesterday</s-option>
    <s-option value="3">Last 7 days</s-option>
    <s-option-group label="Custom ranges">
      <s-option value="4">Last 30 days</s-option>
      <s-option value="5">Last 90 days</s-option>
    </s-option-group>
  </s-select>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: A simple select dropdown with pre-selected value for product sorting options.
examples.jsx:
<s-select label="Sort products by" value="newest">
    <s-option value="newest">Newest first</s-option>
    <s-option value="oldest">Oldest first</s-option>
    <s-option value="title">Title A–Z</s-option>
    <s-option value="price-low">Price: low to high</s-option>
    <s-option value="price-high">Price: high to low</s-option>
  </s-select>
examples.html:
<s-select label="Sort products by" value="newest">
    <s-option value="newest">Newest first</s-option>
    <s-option value="oldest">Oldest first</s-option>
    <s-option value="title">Title A–Z</s-option>
    <s-option value="price-low">Price: low to high</s-option>
    <s-option value="price-high">Price: high to low</s-option>
  </s-select>

examples.title: With placeholder
examples.description: Select dropdown with helpful placeholder text guiding category selection.
examples.jsx:
<s-select

name: Switch
description: Give users a clear way to toggle options on or off.

properties.name: accessibilityLabel
properties.description: A label used for users using assistive technologies like screen readers. When set, any children or `label` supplied will not be announced. This can also be used to display a control without a visual label, while still providing context to users using screen readers.

properties.name: checked
properties.description: Whether the control is active.

properties.name: defaultChecked
properties.description: Whether the control is active by default.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Visual content to use as the control label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The value used in form data when the checkbox is checked.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-switch
    label="Enable feature"
    details="Ensure all criteria are met before enabling"
   />
examples.html:
<s-switch
    label="Enable feature"
    details="Ensure all criteria are met before enabling"
  ></s-switch>

examples.title: Basic switch
examples.description: Standard toggle switch for enabling or disabling merchant preferences. This example demonstrates a simple switch with a label, allowing users to toggle a single setting on or off.
examples.jsx: <s-switch id="basic-switch" label="Enable notifications" />
examples.html: <s-switch id="basic-switch" label="Enable notifications"></s-switch>

examples.title: Disabled switch
examples.description: Locked switch with explanatory text for unavailable premium features. This example shows a switch that is visually disabled and cannot be interacted with, typically used to indicate a feature is not currently available.
examples.jsx:
<s-switch
    id="disabled-switch"
    label="Feature locked (Premium plan required)"
    checked={true}
    disabled={true}
   />
examples.html:
<s-switch
    id="disabled-switch"
    label="Feature locked (Premium plan required)"
    checked="true"
    disabled="true"
  ></s-switch>

examples.title: Form integration
examples.description: Multiple switches within a form for notification preferences submission. This example illustrates how switches can be used together in a form to allow users to configure multiple related settings simultaneously.
examples.jsx:
<form>
    <s-switch
      id="email-notifications"
      label="Email notifications"
      name="emailNotifications"
      value="enabled"
     />
    <s-switch
      id="sms-notifications"
      label="SMS notifications"
      name="smsNotifications"
      value="enabled"
     />
  </form>
examples.html:
<form>
    <s-switch
      id="email-notifications"
      label="Email notifications"
      name="emailNotifications"
      value="enabled"
    ></s-switch>
    <s-switch
      id="sms-notifications"
      label="SMS notifications"
      name="smsNotifications"
      value="enabled"
    ></s-switch>
  </form>

examples.title: Hidden label for accessibility
examples.description: Switch with visually hidden label that remains accessible to screen readers. This example demonstrates how to create a switch with a label that is only perceivable by assistive technologies, maintaining accessibility while minimizing visual clutter.
examples.jsx:
<s-switch
    id="hidden-label-switch"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    label="Toggle feature"
    checked={true}
   />
examples.html:
<s-switch
    id="hidden-label-switch"
    labelAccessibilityVisibility="exclusive"
    label="Toggle feature"
    checked="true"
  ></s-switch>

examples.title: With details and error
examples.description: Required switch with validation error and contextual details for user guidance. This example shows a switch that requires user interaction, provides additional context through details, and displays an error message when validation fails.
examples.jsx:
<s-switch
    id="terms-switch"
    label="Agree to terms and conditions"
    details="You must agree to continue with the purchase"
    error="Agreement is required"
    name="termsAgreement"
    required={true}
    value="agreed"
   />
examples.html:
<s-switch
    id="terms-switch"
    label="Agree to terms and conditions"
    details="You must agree to continue with the purchase"
    error="Agreement is required"
    name="termsAgreement"
    required="true"
    value="agreed"
  ></s-switch>

examples.title: Switch with accessibility label
examples.description: Switch with enhanced accessibility description for screen reader users. This example illustrates how to provide a more descriptive accessibility label that provides additional context beyond the visible label.
examples.jsx:
<s-switch
    id="event-switch"
    label="Feature toggle"
    accessibilityLabel="Toggle feature on or off"
   />
examples.html:
<s-switch
    id="event-switch"
    label="Feature toggle"
    accessibilityLabel="Toggle feature on or off"
  ></s-switch>

examples.title: Settings panel with Stack
examples.description: Group of related switches arranged in a vertical stack for settings configuration. This example demonstrates how to use the Stack component to create a clean, organized layout for multiple related switch settings.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="small-200">
    <s-switch id="notifications-setting" label="Push notifications" />
    <s-switch id="autosave-setting" label="Auto-save drafts" />
    <s-switch
      id="analytics-setting"
      label="Usage analytics"
      checked={true}
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <s-switch id="notifications-setting" label="Push notifications"></s-switch>
    <s-switch id="autosave-setting" label="Auto-save drafts"></s-switch>
    <s-switch
      id="analytics-setting"
      label="Usage analytics"
      checked="true"
    ></s-switch>
  </s-stack>

properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: maxLength
properties.description: Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.

properties.name: minLength
properties.description: Specifies the min number of characters allowed.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: rows
properties.description: A number of visible text lines.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-text-area
    label="Shipping address"
    value="1776 Barnes Street, Orlando, FL 32801"
    rows={3}
   />
examples.html:
<s-text-area
    label="Shipping address"
    value="1776 Barnes Street, Orlando, FL 32801"
    rows="3"
  ></s-text-area>

examples.title: Basic usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple text area for collecting product descriptions with a placeholder and autocomplete disabled.
examples.jsx:
<s-text-area
    label="Product description"
    placeholder="Enter a detailed description..."
    autocomplete="off"
   />
examples.html:
<s-text-area
    label="Product description"
    placeholder="Enter a detailed description..."
    autocomplete="off"
  ></s-text-area>

examples.title: Seo meta description with character limit
examples.description: Showcases a text area for writing SEO meta descriptions with a character limit of 160, providing guidance text and a multi-line input.
examples.jsx:
<s-text-area
    label="Meta description"
    max-length={160}
    details="Appears in search results. Keep under 160 characters for best visibility."
    placeholder="Write a compelling description that will appear in Google search results..."
    rows={3}
    autocomplete="off"
   />
examples.html:
<s-text-area
    label="Meta description"
    max-length="160"
    details="Appears in search results. Keep under 160 characters for best visibility."
    placeholder="Write a compelling description that will appear in Google search results..."
    rows="3"
    autocomplete="off"
  ></s-text-area>

examples.title: With error state
examples.description: Demonstrates the error state of a text area with a minimum length requirement, showing how validation errors are displayed in a commerce context.
examples.jsx:
<s-text-area
    label="Reason for return"
    error="Please provide a detailed explanation for the return request. This helps us improve our products and process the refund faster."
    minLength={20}
    placeholder="Explain why the customer is returning this item..."
    rows={3}
    autocomplete="off"
   />
examples.html:
<s-text-area
    label="Reason for return"
    error="Please provide a detailed explanation for the return request. This helps us improve our products and process the refund faster."
    minLength="20"
    placeholder="Explain why the customer is returning this item..."
    rows="3"
    autocomplete="off"
  ></s-text-area>

examples.title: Product care instructions
examples.description: Illustrates a text area for entering detailed product care instructions, with an expanded height and supporting guidance text.
examples.jsx:
<s-text-area
    label="Care instructions"
    rows={6}
    details="Detailed care instructions help customers maintain their purchases and reduce returns."
    placeholder="Provide washing, storage, and maintenance instructions..."
    autocomplete="off"
   />
examples.html:
<s-text-area
    label="Care instructions"
    rows="6"
    details="Detailed care instructions help customers maintain their purchases and reduce returns."
    placeholder="Provide washing, storage, and maintenance instructions..."
    autocomplete="off"
  ></s-text-area>

properties.description:
A hint as to the intended content of the field.

When set to `on` (the default), this property indicates that the field should support autofill, but you do not have any more semantic information on the intended contents.

When set to `off`, you are indicating that this field contains sensitive information, or contents that are never saved, like one-time codes.

Alternatively, you can provide value which describes the specific data you would like to be entered into this field during autofill.

properties.name: defaultValue
properties.description: The default value for the field.

properties.name: details
properties.description:
Additional text to provide context or guidance for the field. This text is displayed along with the field and its label to offer more information or instructions to the user.

This will also be exposed to screen reader users.

properties.name: disabled
properties.description: Disables the field, disallowing any interaction.

properties.name: error
properties.description: Indicate an error to the user. The field will be given a specific stylistic treatment to communicate problems that have to be resolved immediately.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The type of icon to be displayed in the field.

properties.name: id
properties.description: A unique identifier for the element.

properties.name: label
properties.description: Content to use as the field label.

properties.name: labelAccessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the component's label.

* `visible`: the label is visible to all users.
* `exclusive`: the label is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: maxLength
properties.description: Specifies the maximum number of characters allowed.

properties.name: minLength
properties.description: Specifies the min number of characters allowed.

properties.name: name
properties.description: An identifier for the field that is unique within the nearest containing form.

properties.name: placeholder
properties.description: A short hint that describes the expected value of the field.

properties.name: prefix
properties.description:
A value to be displayed immediately before the editable portion of the field.

This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "https://" or "+353".

This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field.

It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the prefix until the user focuses the input.

properties.name: readOnly
properties.description: The field cannot be edited by the user. It is focusable will be announced by screen readers.

properties.name: required
properties.description: Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the `error` property.

properties.name: suffix
properties.description:
A value to be displayed immediately after the editable portion of the field.

This is useful for displaying an implied part of the value, such as "@shopify.com", or "%".

This cannot be edited by the user, and it isn't included in the value of the field.

It may not be displayed until the user has interacted with the input. For example, an inline label may take the place of the suffix until the user focuses the input.

properties.name: value
properties.description: The current value for the field. If omitted, the field will be empty.

slots.name: accessory
slots.description: The accessory to display in the text field.

examples.title: Specific error messages for merchant context
examples.description: Demonstrates the importance of providing clear, actionable, and context-specific error messages that guide merchants toward correct input and understanding.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="base">
    {/* Generic error (avoid) */}
    <s-text-field label="Product weight" error="Invalid value" />

    {/* Specific error (preferred) */}
    <s-text-field
      label="Product weight"
      error="Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations"
     />

    {/* Business rule error */}
    <s-text-field
      label="SKU"
      error="SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products."
     />
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="base">
    <!-- Generic error (avoid) -->
    <s-text-field label="Product weight" error="Invalid value"></s-text-field>

    <!-- Specific error (preferred) -->
    <s-text-field
      label="Product weight"
      error="Weight must be greater than 0 and less than 500 pounds for shipping calculations"
    ></s-text-field>

    <!-- Business rule error -->
    <s-text-field
      label="SKU"
      error="SKU 'TSHIRT-001' already exists. SKUs must be unique across all products."
    ></s-text-field>
  </s-stack>
