      </s-chip>
    </s-stack>
  </s-box>

bestPractices: `subdued`: use for secondary or less important information
`base`: use as default color
`strong`: use for important or verified status
Text truncates automatically, keep labels short to avoid truncation
Chips are static indicators, not interactive or dismissible. For interactive chips, use ClickableChip
Add icons to `graphic` slot to provide visual context
Display chips near the content they classify
contentGuidelines: Be short and concise to avoid truncation
Use `accessibilityLabel` to describe purpose for screen readers
Common status labels: `Active`, `Draft`, `Published`, `Verified`
Common category labels: `Product type`, `Collection`, `Tag name`
usefulFor: Labeling, organizing, and categorizing objects
Highlighting content attributes
Enhancing discoverability by identifying items with similar properties

name: Heading
description: Renders hierarchical titles to communicate the structure and organization of page content. Heading levels adjust automatically based on nesting within parent Section components, ensuring a meaningful and accessible page outline.

properties.name: accessibilityRole
properties.description:
Sets the semantic meaning of the component’s content. When set, the role will be used by assistive technologies to help users navigate the page.

* `heading`: defines the element as a heading to a page or section.
* `presentation`: the heading level will be stripped, and will prevent the element’s implicit ARIA semantics from being exposed to the accessibility tree.
* `none`: a synonym for the `presentation` role.

properties.name: accessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the element.

* `visible`: the element is visible to all users.
* `hidden`: the element is removed from the accessibility tree but remains visible.
* `exclusive`: the element is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: lineClamp
properties.description: Truncates the text content to the specified number of lines.

slots.name: children
slots.description:
The content of the Heading.

Examples

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx: <s-heading>Online store dashboard</s-heading>
examples.html: <s-heading>Online store dashboard</s-heading>

examples.title: Basic heading
examples.description: Standard heading for section titles and page content organization that creates a simple, clean title for a content section.
examples.jsx: <s-heading>Product details</s-heading>
examples.html: <s-heading>Product details</s-heading>

examples.title: Heading with line clamping
examples.description: Truncated heading that limits text to a specified number of lines, using ellipsis to indicate additional content for long product names or constrained layouts.
examples.jsx:
<s-box inlineSize="200px">
    <s-heading lineClamp={2}>
      Premium organic cotton t-shirt with sustainable manufacturing practices
    </s-heading>
  </s-box>
examples.html:
<s-box inlineSize="200px">
    <s-heading lineClamp="2">
      Premium organic cotton t-shirt with sustainable manufacturing practices
    </s-heading>
  </s-box>

examples.title: Heading with custom accessibility
examples.description: Heading configured with custom ARIA roles and visibility settings to meet specific accessibility requirements or design constraints.
examples.jsx:
<s-heading accessibilityRole="presentation" accessibilityVisibility="hidden">
    Sale badge
  </s-heading>
examples.html:
<s-heading accessibilityRole="presentation" accessibilityVisibility="hidden">
    Sale badge
  </s-heading>

examples.title: Heading within section hierarchy
examples.description: Demonstrates nested heading structure that automatically adjusts heading levels (h2, h3, h4) based on the current section depth, ensuring proper semantic document structure.
examples.jsx:
<s-section>
    <s-heading>Order information</s-heading>
    {/* Renders as h2 */}
    <s-section>
      <s-heading>Shipping details</s-heading>
      {/* Renders as h3 */}
      <s-section>
        <s-heading>Tracking updates</s-heading>
        {/* Renders as h4 */}
      </s-section>
    </s-section>
  </s-section>
examples.html:
<s-section>
    <s-heading>Order information</s-heading>
    <!-- Renders as h2 -->
    <s-section>
      <s-heading>Shipping details</s-heading>
      <!-- Renders as h3 -->
      <s-section>
        <s-heading>Tracking updates</s-heading>
        <!-- Renders as h4 -->
      </s-section>
    </s-section>
  </s-section>

bestPractices: Use short headings to make your content scannable.
Use plain and clear terms.
Don't use jargon or technical language.
Don't use different terms to describe the same thing.
Don't duplicate content.
usefulFor: Creating titles and subtitles for your content that are consistent across your app.
Helping users with visual impairments navigate through content effectively using assistive technologies like screen readers.

name: Paragraph
description: Displays a block of text and can contain inline elements such as buttons, links, or emphasized text. Use to present standalone blocks of content as opposed to inline text.

properties.name: accessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the element.

* `visible`: the element is visible to all users.
* `hidden`: the element is removed from the accessibility tree but remains visible.
* `exclusive`: the element is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: color
properties.description: Modify the color to be more or less intense.

properties.name: dir
properties.description:
Indicates the directionality of the element’s text.

* `ltr`: languages written from left to right (e.g. English)
* `rtl`: languages written from right to left (e.g. Arabic)
* `auto`: the user agent determines the direction based on the content
* `''`: direction is inherited from parent elements (equivalent to not setting the attribute)

properties.name: fontVariantNumeric
properties.description: Set the numeric properties of the font.

properties.name: lineClamp
properties.description: Truncates the text content to the specified number of lines.

properties.name: tone
properties.description: Sets the tone of the component, based on the intention of the information being conveyed.

slots.name: children
slots.description:
The content of the Paragraph.

Examples

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph>
    Shopify POS is the easiest way to sell your products in person. Available for
    iPad, iPhone, and Android.
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph>
    Shopify POS is the easiest way to sell your products in person. Available for
    iPad, iPhone, and Android.
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple paragraph with default styling, showing how to use the paragraph component for standard text content.
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph>
    Track inventory across all your retail locations in real-time.
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph>
    Track inventory across all your retail locations in real-time.
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: With Tone and Color
examples.description: Illustrates how to apply different tones and color variations to convey different types of information, such as informational and success messages.
examples.jsx:
<s-section>
    <s-paragraph tone="info" color="base">
      Your order will be processed within 2-3 business days.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="success" color="subdued">
      Payment successfully processed.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-section>
examples.html:
<s-section>
    <s-paragraph tone="info" color="base">
      Your order will be processed within 2-3 business days.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="success" color="subdued">
      Payment successfully processed.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-section>

examples.title: Line Clamping
examples.description: Shows how to limit the number of lines displayed using the lineClamp prop, which truncates long text with an ellipsis after the specified number of lines.
examples.jsx:
<s-box inlineSize="300px">
    <s-paragraph lineClamp={1}>
      Premium organic cotton t-shirt featuring sustainable manufacturing
      processes, ethically sourced materials, and carbon-neutral shipping.
      Available in multiple colors and sizes with customization options for your
      brand.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-box>
examples.html:
<s-box inlineSize="300px">
    <s-paragraph lineClamp="1">
      Premium organic cotton t-shirt featuring sustainable manufacturing
      processes, ethically sourced materials, and carbon-neutral shipping.
      Available in multiple colors and sizes with customization options for your
      brand.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-box>

examples.title: Tabular Numbers
examples.description: Demonstrates the use of tabular numbers with fontVariantNumeric, ensuring consistent alignment and readability for numerical data.
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph fontVariantNumeric="tabular-nums">
    Orders: 1,234 Revenue: $45,678.90 Customers: 890
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph fontVariantNumeric="tabular-nums">
    Orders: 1,234 Revenue: $45,678.90 Customers: 890
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: RTL Support
examples.description: Illustrates right-to-left (RTL) text rendering, showing how the paragraph component supports internationalization and different text directions.
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph dir="rtl">
    محتوى النص باللغة العربية
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph dir="rtl">
    محتوى النص باللغة العربية
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: Screen Reader Text
examples.description: Shows how to use the accessibilityVisibility prop to create text that is exclusively available to screen readers, improving accessibility for assistive technologies.
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph accessibilityVisibility="exclusive">
    Table sorted by date, newest first.
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph accessibilityVisibility="exclusive">
    Table sorted by date, newest first.
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: Admin UI Patterns
examples.description: Showcases various tone and color combinations for different administrative messages, illustrating how paragraph can communicate different types of information in a user interface.
examples.jsx:
<s-section>
    <s-paragraph tone="success" color="base">
      Payment successfully processed and order confirmed.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="warning" color="base">
      Inventory levels are running low for this product.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="critical" color="base">
      This order requires immediate attention due to shipping delays.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="info" color="base">
      Customer requested gift wrapping for this order.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="caution" color="base">
      Review shipping address before processing.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-section>
examples.html:
<s-section>
    <s-paragraph tone="success" color="base">
      Payment successfully processed and order confirmed.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="warning" color="base">
      Inventory levels are running low for this product.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="critical" color="base">
      This order requires immediate attention due to shipping delays.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="info" color="base">
      Customer requested gift wrapping for this order.
    </s-paragraph>

    <s-paragraph tone="caution" color="base">
      Review shipping address before processing.
    </s-paragraph>
  </s-section>

bestPractices: Use short paragraphs to make your content scannable.
Use plain and clear terms.
Don't use jargon or technical language.
Don't use different terms to describe the same thing.
Don't duplicate content.
usefulFor: Displaying text content in a paragraph format.
Grouping elements with the same style. For instance, icons inside a paragraph will automatically adopt the paragraph's tone.

name: Text
description: Displays inline text with specific visual styles or tones. Use to emphasize or differentiate words or phrases within a Paragraph or other block-level components.

properties.name: accessibilityVisibility
properties.description:
Changes the visibility of the element.

* `visible`: the element is visible to all users.
* `hidden`: the element is removed from the accessibility tree but remains visible.
* `exclusive`: the element is visually hidden but remains in the accessibility tree.

properties.name: color
properties.description: Modify the color to be more or less intense.

properties.name: dir
properties.description:
Indicates the directionality of the element’s text.

* `ltr`: languages written from left to right (e.g. English)
* `rtl`: languages written from right to left (e.g. Arabic)
* `auto`: the user agent determines the direction based on the content
* `''`: direction is inherited from parent elements (equivalent to not setting the attribute)

properties.name: fontVariantNumeric
properties.description: Set the numeric properties of the font.

properties.name: interestFor
properties.description: ID of a component that should respond to interest (e.g. hover and focus) on this component.

properties.name: tone
properties.description: Sets the tone of the component, based on the intention of the information being conveyed.

properties.name: type
properties.description:
Provide semantic meaning and default styling to the text.

Other presentation properties on Text override the default styling.

slots.name: children
slots.description:
The content of the Text.

Examples

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<s-paragraph>
    <s-text type="strong">Name: </s-text>
    <s-text>Jane Doe</s-text>
  </s-paragraph>
examples.html:
<s-paragraph>
    <s-text type="strong">Name: </s-text>
    <s-text>Jane Doe</s-text>
  </s-paragraph>

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Standard text content for general interface messaging and descriptions.
examples.jsx:
<s-text>
    Manage your products and inventory from one dashboard.
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text>
    Manage your products and inventory from one dashboard.
  </s-text>

examples.title: Strong Text
examples.description: Emphasized text for important messages and call-to-actions.
examples.jsx:
<s-text type="strong">
    Free shipping on orders over $50
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text type="strong">
    Free shipping on orders over $50
  </s-text>

examples.title: Semantic Address
examples.description: Structured address text with proper semantic meaning for screen readers.
examples.jsx:
<s-text type="address">
    123 Commerce Street, Toronto, ON M5V 2H1
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text type="address">
    123 Commerce Street, Toronto, ON M5V 2H1
  </s-text>

examples.title: Tabular Numbers
examples.description: Monospace number formatting for consistent alignment in tables and financial data.
examples.jsx:
<s-text fontVariantNumeric="tabular-nums">
    $1,234.56
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text fontVariantNumeric="tabular-nums">
    $1,234.56
  </s-text>

examples.title: Status Tones
examples.description: Color-coded text indicating different status states and semantic meanings.
examples.jsx:
<s-stack gap="small">
    <s-text tone="success">Order fulfilled</s-text>
    <s-text tone="critical">Payment failed</s-text>
    <s-text tone="warning">Low inventory</s-text>
  </s-stack>
examples.html:
<s-stack gap="small">
    <s-text tone="success">Order fulfilled</s-text>
    <s-text tone="critical">Payment failed</s-text>
    <s-text tone="warning">Low inventory</s-text>
  </s-stack>

examples.title: Accessibility Hidden Text
examples.description: Text visible only to screen readers for providing additional context.
examples.jsx:
<s-text accessibilityVisibility="exclusive">
    Product prices include tax
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text accessibilityVisibility="exclusive">
    Product prices include tax
  </s-text>

examples.title: Right-to-Left Text
examples.description: Text direction support for RTL languages like Arabic and Hebrew.
examples.jsx:
<s-text dir="rtl">
    محتوى النص باللغة العربية
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text dir="rtl">
    محتوى النص باللغة العربية
  </s-text>

examples.title: Subdued Color
examples.description: Lower contrast text for secondary information and timestamps.
examples.jsx:
<s-text color="subdued">
    Last updated 2 hours ago
  </s-text>
examples.html:
<s-text color="subdued">
    Last updated 2 hours ago
  </s-text>

examples.title: Interest For Association
examples.description: Text element associated with tooltips using the \`interestFor\` attribute to show additional information on hover or focus.
examples.jsx:
<>
    <s-tooltip id="sku-tooltip">
      SKU must be unique across all products and cannot be changed after creation
    </s-tooltip>
    <s-text color="subdued" interestFor="sku-tooltip">
      What is a product SKU?
    </s-text>
  </>
examples.html:
<s-tooltip id="sku-tooltip">
    SKU must be unique across all products and cannot be changed after creation
  </s-tooltip>
  <s-text color="subdued" interestFor="sku-tooltip">
    What is a product SKU?
  </s-text>

bestPractices: Text elements display inline and will flow on the same line when placed next to each other. To stack multiple text elements vertically, wrap them in a Stack container or use multiple `s-paragraph` components.
Use plain and clear terms.
Don't use jargon or technical language.
Don't use different terms to describe the same thing.
Don't duplicate content.
usefulFor: Adding inline text elements such as labels or line errors.
Applying different visual tones and text styles to specific words or phrases within a `s-paragraph`, such as a `strong` type or `critical` tone.

name: Tooltip
description: Displays helpful information in a small overlay when users hover or focus on an element. Use to provide additional context without cluttering the interface.

slots.name: children
slots.description:
The content of the Tooltip.

Only accepts `Text`, `Paragraph` components, and raw `textContent`.

Examples

examples.title: Code
examples.jsx:
<>
    <s-tooltip id="bold-tooltip">Bold</s-tooltip>
    <s-button interestFor="bold-tooltip" accessibilityLabel="Bold">
      B
    </s-button>
  </>
examples.html:
<s-tooltip id="bold-tooltip">Bold</s-tooltip>
  <s-button interestFor="bold-tooltip" accessibilityLabel="Bold">B</s-button>

examples.title: Basic Usage
examples.description: Demonstrates a simple tooltip that provides additional context for a text element when hovered or focused.
examples.jsx:
<>
    <s-tooltip id="shipping-status-tooltip">
      <s-text>This order has shipping labels.</s-text>
    </s-tooltip>
    <s-text interestFor="shipping-status-tooltip">Shipping status</s-text>
  </>
examples.html:
<s-tooltip id="shipping-status-tooltip">
    <s-text>This order has shipping labels.</s-text>
  </s-tooltip>
  <s-text interestFor="shipping-status-tooltip">Shipping status</s-text>

examples.title: With Icon Button
examples.description: Shows how to add a tooltip to an icon button, providing a clear explanation of the button's action when hovered.
examples.jsx:
<>
    <s-tooltip id="delete-button-tooltip">
      <s-text>Delete item permanently</s-text>
    </s-tooltip>
    <s-button interestFor="delete-button-tooltip">
      <s-icon tone="neutral" type="info" />
    </s-button>
  </>
examples.html:
<s-tooltip id="delete-button-tooltip">
    <s-text>Delete item permanently</s-text>
  </s-tooltip>
  <s-button interestFor="delete-button-tooltip">
    <s-icon tone="neutral" type="info"></s-icon>
  </s-button>

bestPractices: Use for additional, non-essential context only
Provide for icon-only buttons or buttons with keyboard shortcuts
Keep content concise and in sentence case
Don't use for critical information, errors, or blocking messages
Don't contain any links or buttons
Use sparingly. If you need many tooltips, clarify the design and language instead
